entries.title: About the Course
entries.content:
This course introduces students to John Bunyan's classic work, Pilgrim's Progress, building their vocabulary and furnishing their imaginations along the way.

This topic is included in the following course(s): Literature: Grade 2

entries.title: Placement & Combining Tips
entries.content: Students in Grades 1-3 can share these lessons if combining.

Permission to print for non-commercial use. See Alveary group use policy to use lessons
in a group context.

LINKS: Click text or scan the QR code in the top corner of the lesson plan pages to view
online resources associated with the lessons.

Responsibility for previewing all links rests with the teacher. All links were checked at the
time of publication; however, websites change frequently and may contain objectionable
content. Please report broken links by contacting us through our website.

Literature: Grade 2
NOTE: We have noticed that some books with the same ISBN have slightly different pagination. If the pagination in your book does not exactly match the lesson plans, please go by the beginning/ending quotes. There is no need to let us know about wrong page numbers.

☐ As desired, plan for a read-aloud time. Suggestions are provided in the Community Read Aloud Quick Link, but local favorites are highly recommended whenever possible.

☐ The right-hand column of the lesson plans includes teacher notes and ideas to dive deeper into the lesson. Activities such as an "ILLUSTRATE" prompt are mainly intended as afternoon occupations, but they can be included during the lesson if time allows.

blocks.text:
• Read through the lesson and gather any materials or find any necessary links. 

• If you have not already read the book, you may want to read the selected pages in advance.

• Connect back to the previous lesson. Ask students to give a summary of what they remember. (Full narration is not necessary.)

• If students have difficulty recalling, teachers might try showing them a picture from the reading, sharing something that the teacher remembers, or prompting them with the first sentence of the last reading. This will often refresh their memory enough to allow them to recall.

• If students still cannot recall anything from the last reading, then teachers might consider continuing anyway (with a shorter reading this time) or repeating the last lesson, depending on the reading and the nature of the book.

• Optional: use a picture, map, or engaging question to pique the student's interest in the day's lesson. 

• If necessary, you may talk about one or two unfamiliar words that will be crucial to understanding.

• Read the day's passage out loud. When reading to pre-readers, point to the words as you read, even if it seems like they aren't paying attention.

• Move at the student’s pace for the lesson time rather than a particular number of pages. It is vitally important that teachers not try to ‘force feed’ a student more than they can consume in one sitting.

• Students who have a difficult time sitting still or listening may benefit from a fidget of their choice, such as a piece of putty or clay or an afghan; flexible positioning, such as a wobble seat or inverted sitting; or sensory modulation, such as a weighted vest or blanket or adjustment of light, temperature, or sound.

• Have students retell the passage orally.

• Alternatively, learners may use words, pictures, Legos, dolls, etc., to process and convey ideas in the manner most natural to them.  

• Teachers may take turns to model.

• Narration is a complex skill requiring the coordination of several parts of the brain. If a student struggles to narrate in general, with a specific subject or book, or on one day but not others, then this is an opportunity to learn. Observe when they struggle with this or any other activity today. Notice facial expressions and body language; listen to what they say about why it is hard or how they feel about it. Take note of these observations for later, tell them that you see how hard they are working, and then offer whatever support they need to end on a positive note. If necessary, take a break for the day.

• Give the student an opportunity to share thoughts on the lesson and any connections made.  

• To stimulate discussion, you could ask them what they thought about the passage or ask an open-ended question.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 1
lessons.body:
Christian Reading His Book
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ INTRO
This story is an allegory, which is a type of story that uses characters and events to represent ideas and teach a lesson. 

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The First Stage" p.1-6 "As I walked" - "in my book."

• Look at Obstinate in the picture on p.7. How do you feel when you make that face? What do you think Obstinate's name might mean? [stubborn].
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 2
lessons.body:
Christian Fell Into the Slough
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ INTRO
Do you know what a slough is? Christian will fall into a slough in today's readings. [View picture on p.12 after reading.]

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The First Stage" p.6-13 "Tush. Obstinate held" - "all they can."

• Today, we met Pliable. Something that is "pliable" bends easily. Why do you think the author named this character Pliable?
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 3
lessons.body:
Mr. Worldly Wiseman  
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ INTRO
Someone who is "worldly wise" might be considered knowledgeable and
sophisticated. Let's see if that describes Mr. Worldly Wiseman.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The First Stage" p.13-17 "By the direction" - "from his home."
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 4
lessons.body:
Evangelist Helps Christian
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ INTRO
Today, the Evangelist tells Christian that he has been "deluded" [and made to believe what is not true]. Let's find out how this happened.
Vocab: hypocrite [one who says they believe something, but their behavior does not show it].

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The First Stage" p.17-22 "Flashes of fire" - "Psalm 2:12"
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 5
lessons.body:
Christian Finds the Gate
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The Second Stage" p.23-29 "Then Christian prepared" - "pleading with men."
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 6
lessons.body:
Interpreter Teaches Christian 
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ INTRO
What does an interpreter do? [translates for someone] Listen to see if you can figure out why the author named this character Interpreter.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The Second Stage" p.29-34 "Christian looked from" - "down my name."
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 7
lessons.body:
Three Men 
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The Second Stage" p.34-39 "As soon as" - "you, good Interpreter."
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 8
lessons.body:
Christian's Burden Falls Off
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ INTRO
Today, Christian will meet three men in shackles [chains]. Their names are Simple, Sloth, and Presumption. The author named them after their personalities. See if you can figure out what each word means as we read.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The Third Stage" p.41-48 "Now in my" - "to rise again."
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 9
lessons.body:
Christian Goes to the Palace Lodge
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The Third Stage" p.48-54 "I looked toward" - "such as you."
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 10
lessons.body:
Piety, Prudence, Charity
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ INTRO
Today, Christian will talk with Prudence [cautiousness], Piety [reverence], and Charity [generosity]. See if you can figure out why the author gave these particular names to these characters.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The Third Stage" p.54-58 "Christian bowed his" - "of staying behind."
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 11
lessons.body:
Christian Receives His Armor
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The Third Stage" p.58-63 "Christian gestured" - "have shown me."
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 13
lessons.body:
Valley of Humiliation 
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ INTRO
Have you ever felt humiliated? [very embarrassed or small] Today, Christian will walk through the Valley of Humiliation. What do you think might happen?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The Fourth Stage" p.65-68 "Then Christian headed" - "say and do."
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 14
lessons.body:
Christian Fights Against Apollyon
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ INTRO
Have you ever done something you regretted and asked for forgiveness? Today, we will read how Christian asked for forgiveness.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The Fourth Stage" p.68-72 "Christian glanced at" - "almost whispered."
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 15
lessons.body:
Valley of the Shadow of Death
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ INTRO
Has your shoe ever gotten stuck in the mud? Today, we will read about someone getting stuck in a "quagmire" [a very boggy place].

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The Fourth Stage" p.73-77 "It's dark as" - "wear the crown."
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 16
lessons.body:
Christian Meets Faithful 
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The Fifth Stage" p.79-84 "Now as Christian" - "as I slept."
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER TIP
Teachers may wish to preview this reading.

• ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 17
lessons.body:
Faithful Tells His Story
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The Fifth Stage" p.84-90 "But let me" - "Christ's righteousness, etc."
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 18
lessons.body:
Talkative Joins In
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The Fifth Stage" p.90-95 "Besides, as such" - "remains unclean."
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 19
lessons.body:
Talkative Departs
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ INTRO
Today, Faithful has a hard conversation with a friend, which angers his friend.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The Fifth Stage" p.95-100 "For all I" - "through a wilderness."

• Do you think Faithful was right to speak truthfully with his friend, even if it made him angry?
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 20
lessons.body:
Vanity Fair 
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ INTRO
Have you ever been to a fair? What was it like?
Vocab: vanity [something that people take great pride in, but which does not matter].

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The Sixth Stage" p.101-106 "Now when they" - "Phil. 3:20, 21"
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 21
lessons.body:
Meet Lord Hate-Good
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The Sixth Stage" p.106-111 "One mocking merchant" - "have to say."

• Why do you think the people were so angry with Christian and Faithful?
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 22
lessons.body:
Faithful is Judged-Good 
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The Sixth Stage" p.111-115 "Faithful tipped his" - "you are alive."
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER TIP
Teachers may wish to preview this reading.

• ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 23
lessons.body:
Christian Meets Hopeful 
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The Seventh Stage" p.117-121 "Now in my" - "you an answer."

• Why do you think Christian and Hopeful could not agree to travel with Mr. Byends and the others?
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.body:
Exam Week

➜ EXAMS 
Answer question(s) related to course.

Questions will come from:
Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 25
lessons.body:
Mr. Money-Love's Lesson 
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The Seventh Stage" p.121-127 "First, let's look" - "in their pilgrimage."

• What did you think of Mr. Money-love's lesson and Christian's response?
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 26
lessons.body:
Demas Tempts Christian and Hopeful 
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ INTRO
Today, Christian, Hopeful, and the others will be tempted at the hill of Lucre. "Lucre" means money that has been obtained dishonorably. What do you think might happen?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The Seventh Stage" p.127-131 "Then Christian called" - "their weary spirits."
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 27
lessons.body:
The River of the Water of Life 
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The Seventh Stage" p.131-137 "On the banks" - "the giant's advice?"
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 28
lessons.body:
The Castle of Despair
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The Seventh Stage" p.137-140 "Hopeful let out" - "whose name's Despair."
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 29
lessons.body:
The Shepherds
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The Eighth Stage" p.141-145 "They went until" - "from my dream."
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 30
lessons.body:
Ignorance 
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The Ninth Stage" p.147-152 "I slept and" - "birthright to me?"
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.body:
Little Faith 
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The Ninth Stage" p.152-158 "But Little-Faith" - "pilgrims answered, 'No.'"
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 32
lessons.body:
Mr. Self-will 
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ INTRO
Today, Christian meets Atheist. An atheist is someone who does not believe in God.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The Ninth Stage" p.158-164 "But why not?" - "all my reformations."
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 33
lessons.body:
Faithful Taught Hopeful 
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The Ninth Stage" p.164-170 "How could that" - "thinks very differently."
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 34
lessons.body:
Word of God
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The Ninth Stage" p.170-176 "Yes,' Hopeful returned" - "I'll warrant thee."
lessons.teacherNotes: • ILLUSTRATE - Draw a scene from the story using watercolor or another medium such as chalk, pastels, or crayons.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 35
lessons.body:
The Celestial City
⍞ Materials: Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

➜ INTRO
Christian is finally coming to the end of his journey!

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"The Tenth Stage" p.177-193
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER NOTE
If time allows, finish the book and read the entire final chapter today. Otherwise, read for most of the lesson and leave a minute or two to narrate and finish the book over exam week.

lessons.title: 15m Classic Lit.: Grade 2 - Lesson 36
lessons.body:
Exam Weeck

➜ EXAMS 
Answer question(s) related to course.

Questions will come from:
Pilgrim's Progress Book 1

terms.content: · Pilgrim's Progress Book 1: Tell about or illustrate Christian's discussion with Mr. Worldly Wiseman OR Tell about or illustrate Christian’s burden and how it fell off.
· Pilgrim's Progress Book 1: Tell about or illustrate Talkative OR Tell about or illustrate what happened at Vanity Fair.
· Pilgrim's Progress Book 1: Tell or illustrate the story about Christian and Hopeful falling into a net and how they got out.
